CREATE TABLE regression_data (id INT, independent_var FLOAT, dependent_var FLOAT);
INSERT INTO regression_data (id, independent_var, dependent_var) VALUES (1, 10.0, 5.0), (2, 20.0, 10.0), (3, 30.0, 15.0);
SELECT REGR_AVGY(independent_var, dependent_var) FROM regression_data WHERE independent_var IS NOT NULL AND dependent_var IS NOT NULL;
SELECT AVG(independent_var) FROM (SELECT independent_var, dependent_var FROM regression_data WHERE dependent_var IS NOT NULL) AS subquery;
SELECT REGR_AVGY(sub.independent_var, sub.dependent_var) FROM (SELECT independent_var, dependent_var FROM regression_data WHERE independent_var > 0) AS sub;
CREATE TABLE additional_data (id INT, independent_var FLOAT, dependent_var FLOAT);
INSERT INTO additional_data (id, independent_var, dependent_var) VALUES (1, 5.0, 2.5), (2, 15.0, NULL), (3, 25.0, 12.5);
SELECT REGR_AVGY(rd.independent_var, rd.dependent_var) FROM regression_data rd JOIN additional_data ad ON rd.id = ad.id WHERE rd.independent_var IS NOT NULL AND ad.dependent_var IS NOT NULL;
SELECT REGR_AVGY(independent_var, dependent_var) FROM (SELECT * FROM regression_data UNION SELECT * FROM additional_data) AS combined WHERE combined.independent_var IS NOT NULL;
SELECT REGR_AVGY(independent_var, dependent_var) FROM regression_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM additional_data WHERE independent_var > 10.0);
CREATE TABLE null_data (id INT, independent_var FLOAT, dependent_var FLOAT);
INSERT INTO null_data (id, independent_var, dependent_var) VALUES (1, NULL, 3.0), (2, NULL, NULL);

SELECT REGR_AVGY(independent_var, dependent_var) FROM (SELECT * FROM regression_data UNION ALL SELECT * FROM additional_data UNION ALL SELECT * FROM null_data) AS all_data WHERE independent_var IS NOT NULL;
